# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// main.ts - Deno Deploy application with dynamic module loading plugin system

// Plugin interface that all plugins must implement
interface Plugin {
  name: string;
  version?: string;
  init?: () => Promise<void> | void;
  handle?: (request: Request) => Promise<Response | null> | Response | null;
  transform?: (data: unknown) => Promise<unknown> | unknown;
}

// Plugin registry to keep track of loaded plugins
const pluginRegistry: Map<string, Plugin> = new Map();

// Allowed module sources for security (configurable via env)
function getAllowedSources(): string[] {
  const sources = Deno.env.get("ALLOWED_MODULE_SOURCES");
  if (sources) {
    return sources.split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
  }
  // Default allowed sources
  return [
    "https://deno.land/",
    "https://esm.sh/",
    "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/",
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/",
    "jsr:",
  ];
}

function isModuleAllowed(modulePath: string): boolean {
  const allowedSources = getAllowedSources();
  return allowedSources.some((source) => modulePath.startsWith(source));
}

// Load a plugin module dynamically
async function loadPlugin(modulePath: string): Promise<Plugin | null> {
  // Check if already loaded
  if (pluginRegistry.has(modulePath)) {
    return pluginRegistry.get(modulePath)!;
  }

  // Validate module source
  if (!isModuleAllowed(modulePath)) {
    console.error(
      `Module source not allowed: ${modulePath}. Allowed sources: ${getAllowedSources().join(", ")}`
    );
    return null;
  }

  try {
    console.log(`Loading plugin from: ${modulePath}`);
    const module = await import(modulePath);

    // Check if module exports a default plugin or a plugin object
    const plugin: Plugin = module.default || module.plugin || module;

    if (!plugin.name) {
      console.error(
        `Plugin at ${modulePath} does not export a valid plugin (missing 'name' property)`
      );
      return null;
    }

    // Initialize plugin if it has an init method
    if (plugin.init) {
      await plugin.init();
      console.log(`Plugin "${plugin.name}" initialized successfully`);
    }

    // Register the plugin
    pluginRegistry.set(modulePath, plugin);
    console.log(
      `Plugin "${plugin.name}" v${plugin.version || "unknown"} loaded successfully`
    );

    return plugin;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Failed to load plugin from ${modulePath}:`, error);
    return null;
  }
}

// Load plugins from environment variables
async function loadPluginsFromEnv(): Promise<void> {
  // PLUGINS env var should be a comma-separated list of module URLs
  const pluginPaths = Deno.env.get("PLUGINS");
  if (pluginPaths) {
    const paths = pluginPaths.split(",").map((p) => p.trim()).filter(Boolean);
    for (const path of paths) {
      await loadPlugin(path);
    }
  }

  // Also support individual plugin env vars: PLUGIN_1, PLUGIN_2, etc.
  for (let i = 1; i <= 20; i++) {
    const pluginPath = Deno.env.get(`PLUGIN_${i}`);
    if (pluginPath) {
      await loadPlugin(pluginPath.trim());
    }
  }
}

// Get plugin status information
function getPluginStatus(): object {
  const plugins: Record<string, object> = {};
  for (const [path, plugin] of pluginRegistry) {
    plugins[plugin.name] = {
      path,
      version: plugin.version || "unknown",
      hasHandler: !!plugin.handle,
      hasTransform: !!plugin.transform,
    };
  }
  return {
    totalPlugins: pluginRegistry.size,
    allowedSources: getAllowedSources(),
    plugins,
  };
}

// Process request through plugin pipeline
async function processPluginPipeline(request: Request): Promise<Response | null> {
  for (const [, plugin] of pluginRegistry) {
    if (plugin.handle) {
      try {
        const response = await plugin.handle(request);
        if (response) {
          return response;
        }
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(`Plugin "${plugin.name}" handler error:`, error);
      }
    }
  }
  return null;
}

// Transform data through plugin pipeline
async function transformThroughPlugins(data: unknown): Promise<unknown> {
  let result = data;
  for (const [, plugin] of pluginRegistry) {
    if (plugin.transform) {
      try {
        result = await plugin.transform(result);
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(`Plugin "${plugin.name}" transform error:`, error);
      }
    }
  }
  return result;
}

// Load initial plugins from environment
await loadPluginsFromEnv();

// Main request handler
Deno.serve(async (request: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const path = url.pathname;

  // CORS headers
  const corsHeaders = {
    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, OPTIONS",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type",
  };

  // Handle CORS preflight
  if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
    return new Response(null, { status: 204, headers: corsHeaders });
  }

  try {
    // Route: Load a plugin dynamically via query parameter
    if (path === "/plugins/load" && request.method === "POST") {
      const body = await request.json();
      const modulePath = body.module || url.searchParams.get("module");

      if (!modulePath) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            error: "Missing 'module' parameter. Provide a module URL to load.",
          }),
          {
            status: 400,
            headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }

      const plugin = await loadPlugin(modulePath);

      if (plugin) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            success: true,
            plugin: {
              name: plugin.name,
              version: plugin.version || "unknown",
              hasHandler: !!plugin.handle,
              hasTransform: !!plugin.transform,
            },
          }),
          {
            status: 200,
            headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      } else {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            error: `Failed to load plugin from: ${modulePath}`,
            hint: `Ensure the module URL starts with one of: ${getAllowedSources().join(", ")}`,
          }),
          {
            status: 400,
            headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }
    }

    // Route: Load plugin via GET with query parameter
    if (path === "/plugins/load" && request.method === "GET") {
      const modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module");

      if (!modulePath) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            error: "Missing 'module' query parameter.",
            usage:
              "GET /plugins/load?module=https://esm.sh/your-plugin-module",
          }),
          {
            status: 400,
            headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }

      const plugin = await loadPlugin(modulePath);

      if (plugin) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            success: true,
            plugin: {
              name: plugin.name,
              version: plugin.version || "unknown",
            },
          }),
          {
            status: 200,
            headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      } else {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: `Failed to load plugin from: ${modulePath}` }),
          {
            status: 400,
            headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }
    }

    // Route: Unload a plugin
    if (path === "/plugins/unload" && request.method === "POST") {
      const body = await request.json();
      const modulePath = body.module;

      if (modulePath && pluginRegistry.has(modulePath)) {
        const plugin = pluginRegistry.get(modulePath)!;
        pluginRegistry.delete(modulePath);
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({
            success: true,
            message: `Plugin "${plugin.name}" unloaded`,
          }),
          {
            status: 200,
            headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
          }
        );
      }

      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: "Plugin not found or not loaded" }),
        {
          status: 404,
          headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        }
      );
    }

    // Route: List all loaded plugins
    if (path === "/plugins" && request.method === "GET") {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(getPluginStatus(), null, 2), {
        status: 200,
        headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    // Route: Transform data through plugins
    if (path === "/plugins/transform" && request.method === "POST") {
      const body = await request.json();
      const transformed = await transformThroughPlugins(body.data || body);

      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({
          original: body.data || body,
          transformed,
          pluginsApplied: Array.from(pluginRegistry.values())
            .filter((p) => p.transform)
            .map((p) => p.name),
        }),
        {
          status: 200,
          headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        }
      );
    }

    // Route: Execute plugin handlers on the request
    if (path === "/plugins/execute") {
      const pluginResponse = await processPluginPipeline(request);
      if (pluginResponse) {
        // Add CORS headers to plugin response
        const headers = new Headers(pluginResponse.headers);
        for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(corsHeaders)) {
          headers.set(key, value);
        }
        return new Response(pluginResponse.body, {
          status: pluginResponse.status,
          headers,
        });
      }

      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({
          message: "No plugin handled this request",
          loadedPlugins: Array.from(pluginRegistry.values()).map((p) => p.name),
        }),
        {
          status: 404,
          headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        }
      );
    }

    // Route: Health check
    if (path === "/health") {
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({
          status: "healthy",
          pluginsLoaded: pluginRegistry.size,
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        }),
        {
          status: 200,
          headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        }
      );
    }

    // Default route: Try plugin pipeline first, then show API docs
    const pluginResponse = await processPluginPipeline(request);
    if (pluginResponse) {
      const headers = new Headers(pluginResponse.headers);
      for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(corsHeaders)) {
        headers.set(key, value);
      }
      return new Response(pluginResponse.body, {
        status: pluginResponse.status,
        headers,
      });
    }

    // API documentation
    const apiDocs = {
      name: "Deno Deploy Plugin System",
      description:
        "Dynamically load and manage plugin modules on the edge",
      endpoints: {
        "GET /health": "Health check with plugin count",
        "GET /plugins": "List all loaded plugins and their status",
        "GET /plugins/load?module=<url>":
          "Load a plugin module from URL (GET)",
        "POST /plugins/load": {
          description: "Load a plugin module",
          body: { module: "https://esm.sh/your-plugin-module" },
        },
        "POST /plugins/unload": {
          description: "Unload a plugin",
          body: { module: "https://esm.sh/your-plugin-module" },
        },
        "POST /plugins/transform": {
          description: "Transform data through all plugin transform functions",
          body: { data: "your data here" },
        },
        "ANY /plugins/execute":
          "Route request through plugin handler pipeline",
      },
      configuration: {
        PLUGINS:
          "Comma-separated list of plugin module URLs to load at startup",
        "PLUGIN_1..PLUGIN_20":
          "Individual plugin module URLs to load at startup",
        ALLOWED_MODULE_SOURCES:
          "Comma-separated list of allowed module URL prefixes",
      },
      pluginInterface: {
        name: "string (required)",
        version: "string (optional)",
        "init()": "async function called when plugin is loaded (optional)",
        "handle(request)":
          "async function to handle HTTP requests, return Response or null (optional)",
        "transform(data)":
          "async function to transform data (optional)",
      },
      currentStatus: getPluginStatus(),
    };

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(apiDocs, null, 2), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Request handling error:", error);
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: "Internal server error",
        message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      }),
      {
        status: 500,
        headers: { ...corsHeaders, "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      }
    );
  }
});
